paper_title: Species Distribution and Richness Mapping of Larger Foraminifera

methods.description: Compilation and synonymization of species records for larger benthic foraminifera (LBF) from various oceanic regions to establish a comprehensive dataset for analysis.

steps.description: Sixty-eight validly recognized species of LBF were identified and synonymized for the Indian (including the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf) and Pacific Ocean.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and synonymize 68 LBF species from specified oceanic regions

steps.description: A number of 2,964 occurrence records from 507 sample sites were included for establishing the richness maps.
steps.searchable_summary: Compile 2,964 occurrence records from 507 sample sites

steps.description: The bulk of distributional data have been carefully extracted from a total of 114 literature studies published by generations of micropaleontologists between 1826 and 2017.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract distributional data from 114 literature studies

methods.description: Procedure for creating grid-based range maps and richness maps of Indo-Pacific symbiont-bearing larger foraminifera species using R and ArcMap.

steps.description: Individual grid-based range maps of the Indo-Pacific symbiont-bearing larger foraminifera species were established and subsequently combined to richness maps.
steps.searchable_summary: Create and combine grid-based range maps to form richness maps

steps.description: Two different methods have been applied: (1) an overlay of individual Minimum Convex Polygon (MCP) range maps and (2) an overlay of species ranges as predicted by Species Distribution Models (SDMs).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply MCP and SDM methods to create species range overlays

steps.description: The maps were created using ArcMap 10.3.1 for Desktop and projected onto the WGS 1984 PDC Mercator coordinate system centered at 180° longitude.
steps.searchable_summary: Create maps using ArcMap and project onto WGS 1984 PDC Mercator

steps.description: Coral Reef regions layers from The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species ™ were buffered with a 100 km radius and used as a mask.
steps.searchable_summary: Buffer Coral Reef regions and use as mask for species range projection
